title: ficus hirta var. malayana
intro: Ficus hirta var. malayana, also known as the Malay banyan or fig tree, is a deciduous species of fig tree native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections. The health benefits of Ficus hirta var. malayana include its ability to support the immune system, reduce stress, and promote digestive health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
